topics.headline: California May Revision 2026 — what charter intervention budgets actually lost (and what to do with the next 90 days)
topics.why_matters: Every Tier A partner is a California charter whose categorical and discretionary intervention dollars are downstream of the May Revise. The Revise is the single largest funding signal in the 14-day window and the document Danielle's prospects are reading right now. A+ is uniquely positioned to translate Revise line items into "what this means for your Title I supplement-not-supplant calculus next school year" — not the macro takes EdSource is doing, but the operator-level math.
topics.angle: A short, plainly-written breakdown of the three Revise line items that most affect intervention budgets at independent study charters, with a closing nudge that A+ can help model the next-year supplement budget. No politics; just translation.
topics.roman_take: Conviction angle — "Every May the same conversation: directors read the Revise as a top-line number, panic, and then under-spend in fall because they're protecting cash. The cost of that caution is measurable. Here's the math."
topics.danielle_take: Implementation angle — "Three calls I'm having this week with charter business managers about what's funded vs. unfunded for FY27 intervention plans. Here's the worksheet I'm using."

topics.headline: Antelope Valley Learning Academy enters Year-2 CSI-Grad cycle inside the iLEAD network — what intervention infrastructure is actually moving the graduation indicator?
topics.why_matters: CSI-Grad schools that don't move their indicator in Year 2 face escalating state oversight. AVLA sits inside a network A+ already serves — this is a content moment where A+ can demonstrate it understands the *operational* difference between "we offer tutoring" and "we deliver an ESSA-aligned intervention with attendance, dosage, and outcome reporting that survives an LCAP audit." High differentiation potential.
topics.angle: Frame the structural problem: CSI-Grad designation isn't fixed by adding more tutors — it's fixed by tutoring that produces evidence the state will actually count. Tie to A+'s ESSA Tier 2 evidence base without naming AVLA directly (network sensitivity).
topics.roman_take: "We keep meeting schools who think the CSI exit is a vendor problem. It's a measurement problem. Here's what changes when you treat intervention as an evidence pipeline, not a service contract."
topics.danielle_take: "Three things I'd put on the next board agenda for any school in Year 2 of CSI-Grad — dosage minimums, attendance recovery integration, and quarterly indicator-tied reporting. None of them require new funding. All of them require a partner that can produce the data." ---

topics.headline: AI tutoring pilots are hitting an LTEL-shaped wall — and nobody is talking about it
topics.why_matters: Every competitor announcement this spring is AI-forward. None of them have a published reclassification outcome for LTELs. A+ has documented LTEL reclassification results from real Title III–funded engagements at Ocean Grove, South Sutter, and Visions. This is the cleanest "only A+ can say this" topic available right now — a competitor lens story that lands exactly on A+'s deepest moat.
topics.angle: "AI tutoring can drill vocabulary. It cannot reclassify an LTEL. Here's why the difference is showing up in pilot data."
topics.roman_take: Conviction piece — the intervention market is sorting itself into two lanes (AI for practice, humans for outcomes that get audited), and vendors pretending to do both are about to get exposed.
topics.danielle_take: Implementation reality — what a reclassification-targeted Title III engagement actually looks like week to week, and why no current AI product can replicate the documentation chain a CDE compliance review requires.
